revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:羽田神社]]
{{Confusion|Haneda Kūkō Shrine|x1=Located at Haneda Airport in Ota Ward|link1={{ill|Haneda Kūkō Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haneda Kūkō Shrine|1=Haneda Aviation Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q109784392}}|穴守 Inari Shrine|link2={{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|1=Anamori Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11597221}}|x2=Located in Haneda, Ota Ward}} {{Infobox religious building| |Name = Haneda Shrine |image = File:Haneda Shrine 01.jpg |address = {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}} [[Ota Ward]] [[Hon Haneda]] 3-9-12 |Location = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|32|49|N|139|44|25.4|E|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, {{ill|Inadahime|ja|稲田姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q505469}} |Status = Former Village shrine (guardian shrine of Hon Haneda, Haneda, and Haneda Asahicho) |Established = [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]] |Honden = |Alternate Names = Tenno-san, Yakumo Shrine, Ushiro Tenno Shrine |Temple/Place = |Annual Festival = April 18 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo city|lat_deg=35|lat_min=32|lat_sec=49|lon_deg=139|lon_min=45|lon_sec=25.4|label=Haneda Shrine|position=right|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} |location = {{ill|Ota|qid=Q217234|1=Ōta, Tokyo}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.546949|N|139.740396|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Kushinadahime|qid=Q505469|1=Kushinadahime}}; {{ill|Susanoo|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} }} '''Haneda Shrine''' (はねだじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Gion cult|en|Gion cult|qid=Q11588425|lt=Gion faith|1=Gion Faith}} Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}. It serves as the guardian shrine for the areas corresponding to the former Haneda Village and Haneda Ryōshichō (now [[Hon Haneda]], [[Haneda (Ota Ward)|Haneda]], and [[Haneda Asahicho]]). Its former names include {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11391847}} and {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|qid=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō Shrine|1=Gozu Tennō}}.
== History == {{See also|Hon Haneda|Haneda Town}} During the [[Sengoku period (Japan)|Sengoku period]], the origin of the shrine is attributed to Yukiyoshi Yojirō, the lord of the Kamata, Rokugō, Haneda, and Daishi-gahara areas and the de facto ruler of the Hanedaura naval forces, who enshrined {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|qid=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}.
It is said that the head priest of a "Hachiman Shrine" (now known as {{ill|Shirohata Hachiman Ōjin|ja|白幡八幡大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirohata Hachiman Ōjin|1=Shirahata Hachiman Daijin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11579758}}) located in Hira Village of the Inage territory served as the shrine’s administrator, and that a festival with mikoshi processions was held in June.<ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|date=1830 |publisher=}}</ref> {{Quotation|From Jishiin "Located in the central part of Nishimachi, the area is divided into four sections, three bo and twelve bu. Ushiro Tenno Shrine Located within the precincts. In the area known as Jochi, it measures nine bo and two bu. It serves as the guardian shrine for this village and Ryōshichō. The main shrine is one ken square, with a worship hall measuring three ken by two ken. Two torii gates stand at its entrance. The sacred object is a standing statue of the local Buddha Yakushi Nyorai, approximately three shaku tall. The Koizumi family, serving as the head priest of the Hachiman Shrine in Hira Village of the Inage territory, managed the shrine duties. The festival was held from June 6 to 11 with a mikoshi procession. An Inari shrine, a small auxiliary shrine, is also located within the premises." (from the 13th year of Bunsei, ''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō'')}} In {{ill|1861|en|1861|qid=Q7693|lt=1861|1=1861}} (the first year of {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|qid=Q730266|lt=Bunkyu|1=Bunkyū}}), when smallpox ({{ill|variola|ja|疱瘡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/variola|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然痘|1=Smallpox|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q12214}}) was rampant, the shōgun {{ill|Tokugawa Iesada|en|Tokugawa Iesada|qid=Q336383|lt=Tokugawa Ietsuna|1=Tokugawa Iesada}} visited the shrine to pray for recovery and was said to have been healed. This event enhanced the shrine's reputation for curing illnesses and attracted the devotion of families such as the {{ill|Tokugawa clan|en|Tokugawa clan|qid=Q1412769|lt=Tokugawa|1=Tokugawa clan}}, {{ill|Shimazu clan|en|Shimazu clan|qid=Q1061406|lt=Shimazu|1=Shimazu clan}}, and {{ill|Tōdō clan|en|Tōdō clan|qid=Q11623716|lt=Tōdō|1=Tōdō clan}}.
For a long time, the shrine was enshrined as "Ushiro Tenno Shrine"—the guardian shrine of the neighboring Jishiin—but during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} period, following the policy of separation of Shinto and Buddhism (【神仏分離】), it was reorganized and established as an independent shrine under the Gion faith, enshrining {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} and {{ill|Inadahime|ja|稲田姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q505469}}, and renamed as {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11391847}}.
On [[July 1, 1871]] ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 4, May 14 [old calendar]), under the Taijōkan decree "Rules for Shrines and Shinto Officials, etc., Below the Level of the Official Shrines," it became the village shrine of the area corresponding to the former Haneda Village and Haneda Ryōshichō (now [[Hon Haneda]], [[Haneda (Ota Ward)|Haneda]], and [[Haneda Asahicho]]).
In the early Meiji period, a Fujizuka (mound dedicated to Fuji) was built by a local Fujikō group.
In {{ill|1907|en|1907|qid=Q2048|lt=1907|1=1907}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 40), Haneda Village was granted town status as Haneda Town<ref name="kadokawa2">"Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten" Editorial Committee, ''Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten'', Vol. 13, Tokyo, 1978, ISBN 4040011309</ref> and in the same year the shrine was renamed '''Haneda Shrine'''<ref name="史跡散歩">Yoshiyuki Shinkura, ''Ota Ward Historic Shrine Walk (Tokyo Historic Shrine Guide 11)'', Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 65–66</ref>.
Between {{ill|1945|en|1945|qid=Q5240|lt=1945|1=1945}} (Showa 20) and {{ill|1948|en|1948|qid=Q5165|lt=1948|1=1948}} (Showa 23), due to the expansion of Tokyo International Airport (now Haneda Airport) by the Allied forces, residents of Haneda Anamori Town, Haneda Suzukicho, and Haneda Edomimachi (present-day Haneda Airport) were forced to leave, and the shrine of {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|1=Anamori Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11597221}} was merged with Haneda Shrine. Later, the shrine building was reconstructed within the parish area (Haneda 5-chome). Also, the Suzunō Inari Shrine, which had been forcibly relocated, was moved into the precincts of Haneda Shrine, and the Tamagawa Benzaiten was concurrently relocated to the Haneda Suijin Shrine within the grounds.
== Parish Area == === Haneda Shrine Parish Area === Former Haneda Village / Former Haneda Ryōshichō * Ota Ward, Hon Haneda * Ota Ward, Haneda – In Haneda 5-chome, Anamori Inari Shrine is enshrined. * Ota Ward, Haneda Asahicho === Joint Parish Areas of Haneda Shrine and Its Concurrent Shrines === * Ota Ward, [[Haginaka]] – Parish area of the former Haginaka Village shrine, Haginaka Shrine * Ota Ward, [[Nishikojiyadani]] – Parish area of the former Kojiyadani Village shrine, Kojiyadani Shrine * Ota Ward, Higashi Kojiyadani – Parish area of the former Kojiyadani Village shrine, Kojiyadani Shrine
== Concurrent Shrines and In-Shrine Shrines == === Concurrent Shrines === In the Kojiyadani and Haneda areas, numerous concurrent shrines are present. * Nishi Ichinaka Ena Shrine * Nakachina Shrine * Nishi Naka Shrine * Kojiyadani Shrine – former shrine of Kojiyadani Village * Hamatake Shrine * Santoku Inari Shrine * Muramori Inari Shrine * Haginaka Shrine – former shrine of Haginaka Village * Myōhō Inari Shrine * Jūkō Inari Shrine * Nakamura Tenso Shrine * Aogi Inari Shrine * Shirouo Inari Shrine * Haneda Suijin Shrine === In-Shrine Shrines === * Suzunō Inari Shrine * Masuda Inari Shrine * Haneda Inari Shrine * Hie Shrine * {{ill|Fujizuka|en|Fujizuka|qid=Q5507555|lt=Fujizuka|1=Fujizuka}} "Haneda Fuji" – Built in the early Meiji period and designated as a tangible folk cultural asset of Ota Ward<ref name="大田区">[https://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/shisetsu/rekishi/koujiya_haneda/fujiduka.html "Fujizuka (Haneda Shrine)"] Ota Ward Office</ref>.
== Access == * 9-minute walk from {{ill|Ōtorii Station|en|Ōtorii Station|qid=Q5362449|lt=Ōtorii Station|1=Ōtorii Station}} ({{OSM経路図|start=35.55245,139.73939|goal=35.54693,139.74050|name=Route Guide}}). * 12-minute walk from [[Anamori Inari Station]] ({{OSM経路図|start=35.55041,139.74679|goal=35.54693,139.74050|name=Route Guide}}).
== Footnotes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * Yoshiyuki Shinkura, ''Ota Ward Historic Shrine Walk (Tokyo Historic Shrine Guide 11)'', Gakuseisha, 1992
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Haneda-jinja}} * [[Jishiin (Ota Ward)|Jishiin]] * {{ill|Anamori Inari Shrine|ja|穴守稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anamori Inari Shrine|1=Anamori Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11597221}}
== External Links == * [https://www.hanedajinja.com/ Haneda Shrine] – Official Website
{{translated page|ja|羽田神社|version=103316013|comment=Imported full ja history}}
<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Haneda-jinja}} [[ja:羽田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q97196990}} [[Category:Fujizuka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ota, Tokyo]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
